message:
Improve etcd detection beyond pod name matching (#3700)

EtcdStatus previously only detected etcd by checking if a pod name
contained 'etcd', which missed pods with custom names (e.g. etcd-main-0)
and etcd running outside kube-system. This caused zero members to show
as "managed by provider" even when etcd was present but undetected.

Changes:
- Detect etcd via pod labels (component=etcd, app.kubernetes.io/name=etcd,
  app=etcd+tier=control-plane) and container names (etcd, etcd-container)
- Search all namespaces instead of only kube-system
- Add distinct "not detected" state (pods visible but no etcd found) vs
  "managed by provider" state (no pods visible at all)
- Show per-cluster managed/not-detected status in multi-cluster view

Closes #3676

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1950,6 +1950,8 @@
   "etcdStatus": {
     "managedByProvider": "etcd Managed by Provider",
     "managedDescription": "etcd is managed by your cloud provider or not visible in kube-system",
+    "notDetected": "etcd Not Detected",
+    "notDetectedDescription": "No etcd pods found by name, labels, or container name. etcd may use custom naming or run outside the cluster.",
     "membersSummary": "{{members}} etcd members across {{clusters}} clusters",
     "membersCount": "{{ready}}/{{total}} members",
     "restarts": "{{count}} restarts",
